title: Dormant Weekend Break-In: Strictly Business Card Shows First Saturday Activity
narrative: A corporate purchasing card with 847 historical transactions spanning 19 months suddenly processes its first-ever weekend transactions. The cardholder's established pattern shows exclusively Monday-Friday activity between 8 AM-6 PM EST, averaging $340 per transaction at B2B merchants (MCC 5044, 5065, 5072). On Saturday at 2:17 AM, the card processes $1,847 at an electronics retailer, followed by $923 at a gaming site 14 minutes later, then $2,104 at a luxury goods merchant at 3:51 AM. All three transactions occur during hours the legitimate user has never transacted (card1_txn_count_weekend=0 historically, txn_hour range historically 8-18). The addr1 changes from the business ZIP to a residential location 340 miles away. P_emaildomain switches from corporate domain to generic webmail. Card1_amt_zscore jumps to 4.2 standard deviations above historical mean.
indicators: card1_txn_count_weekend transitions from 0 to 3 in single session after 847 weekday-only transactions
All three transactions occur between 2:00-4:00 AM when historical txn_hour range is 8-18
TransactionAmt values ($1,847, $923, $2,104) exceed historical mean by 4.2 standard deviations
addr1 changes from established business ZIP to location 340 miles distant
P_emaildomain switches from corporate domain pattern to generic webmail provider
MCC shift from B2B categories (5044/5065/5072) to consumer retail (5732/7995/5094)
Transaction velocity of 3 purchases in 94 minutes vs historical average of 1.2 per day
summary: A business card with 847 weekday-only transactions (8 AM-6 PM) suddenly makes its first three weekend purchases at 2-4 AM, with amounts 4.2 standard deviations above normal, shipping to a new address 340 miles away, and the email changing from corporate to personal webmail.
reasoning: Criminals who compromised the card are rushing to extract value during off-hours when the legitimate business owner won't notice, using a different shipping address and personal email to receive goods before the fraud is detected on Monday morning.
indicator_explanations: Business card that never transacted on weekends suddenly fires three Saturday purchases
Transactions hit during graveyard hours when card historically only operates business hours
Purchase amounts are dramatically larger than this card's established spending pattern
Billing address jumps hundreds of miles away from the business location
Email changes from company domain to free consumer email service
Card shifts from wholesale business purchases to consumer electronics and entertainment
Card fires three times in 90 minutes when it normally spaces transactions throughout the day
